summary: Custom-kind units join coverage — read authored `satisfies` from the member document instead of the hardcoded empty vec

edit.description: Populate `Features.satisfies` for a custom-kind unit from the parsed member document's `[satisfies.*]` modules rather than the hardcoded `satisfies: Vec::new()` in `Kind::extract` (kind.rs:737-740 names it a later follow-on). This is NOT a new extraction primitive — the `satisfies` binding is already parsed from the surface header (as for skill/rule via extract); thread it into the composed Unit's Features. Coverage stays kind-blind by design (specs/15-kinds.md worked example — temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements).

Chain the registered custom kinds' features into `all_features` before `coverage::check` (main.rs:624-629, today skill ⊕ rule only). GRAPH-CUSTOM-KIND (8f057c7) already computes the `custom_kinds` features up front and joins them to `by_kind`, so the features are in hand — this only adds them to the coverage chain.
A custom-kind unit declaring `satisfies` covers a requirement, exactly as a skill/rule does.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage (kind-blind).

acceptance: A custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage exactly as a skill/rule does; temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Revived to open: GRAPH-CUSTOM-KIND (8f057c7) shipped, so custom features already join `by_kind`/`edges` — the remaining work is threading `satisfies` (kind.rs:740 still `Vec::new()`) and chaining custom features into `all_features` (main.rs:624, skill⊕rule only). Buildable against fixtures; no in-repo `.temper` needed. Disjoint from the contract.rs source-key entry.

summary: Parse and preserve a per-clause `source` citation key (URL + retrieved date) on the PACKAGE.md loader, beside `guidance`

per.section: Decision: a built-in package is named for its source, and cited to it

edit.description: Add an optional `source` field to the `Clause` struct (contract.rs:81-96), parse it in `parse_clause` (658-668), and whitelist `"source"` in `reject_unknown_clause_keys` (682-691) so it is no longer an `UnknownKey` reject. It is preserved metadata (a citation), not a predicate — no admissibility gate on its content beyond being a recognized key; the spec's clause example carries it beside `guidance`.
A clause carrying `source` loads and round-trips without tripping UnknownKey; a clause without it still loads unchanged.

tests.asserts: a `source` key on a clause parses and is preserved; absence stays valid; an unrelated stray key still rejects.

acceptance: A PACKAGE.md clause may carry a `source` citation beside `guidance`; the loader parses and preserves it; genuinely unknown clause keys still reject; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: From inbox: specs/10-contracts.md ('named for its source, and cited to it') adds a per-clause `source`; the loader must parse/preserve it before the packages/ std-lib authoring session (packages/DOSSIER.md consumes it). On disk: contract.rs `Clause` has only severity/predicate/guidance and `reject_unknown_clause_keys` whitelists exactly those + arg keys, so `source` rejects today. contract.rs-only; disjoint from COVERAGE (kind.rs/main.rs).

summary: Relicense to dual MIT OR Apache-2.0 — add LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE and set the Cargo.toml `license` field, before external contributors

per.section: Decision: the license is MIT OR Apache-2.0, dual, before launch

new.description: The standard Apache-2.0 license text (the patent grant the dual choice buys).

edit.description: Set `license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"` (the SPDX expression the dual grant requires; the API-guidelines / uv / Biome choice). The Cargo.toml `license` field follows the LICENSE change, per the Decision.

retire: LICENSE (the bare-MIT root file the dual grant supersedes, if present)

acceptance: The repo carries LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE, the bare MIT LICENSE is retired, and Cargo.toml declares `license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"`; cargo build/fmt/clippy green.
notes: From the offering tranche (specs/55-offering.md). Do the relicense now — the Decision's whole point is 'while there are no external contributors' (clean git status confirms the window). SPDX `MIT OR Apache-2.0`. Disjoint from all other open entries: owns the Cargo.toml `license` field and the LICENSE-* files, touches no source and no README.

summary: Author the launch community docs — CONTRIBUTING.md (two-sided AI policy), SECURITY.md (evidence bar), and bug/feature issue forms

per.section: The community surface — small, honest, gated

new.description: Small and honest: file-a-good-issue, search-first, PRs-by-prior-discussion, and the two-sided AI-authorship policy (outbound — this codebase is agent-built under human specs, stated as fact; inbound — AI-assisted contributions welcome WITH disclosure, contributor must defend the change without the assistant), per the AI-authorship Decision.
Private vulnerability reporting plus an explicit evidence bar — a report must demonstrate, not speculate (the unverified-AI-report spam lesson).
A bug issue form: version, OS, repro.
Optional, if trivial: disable blank issues so reports route through the forms.

acceptance: CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, and one bug + one feature issue form exist per the community-surface + AI-authorship Decisions; no code surface changes; cargo green.
notes: From the offering tranche (specs/55-offering.md). Scope to the launch set ONLY — deliberately NOT GitHub Discussions, a code of conduct, or good-first-issue bait (the Decision defers/rejects them). Enabling private vuln reporting is a GitHub repo setting (human), not a file; SECURITY.md carries the policy + evidence bar. Docs-only; disjoint from LICENSE/README/code entries.

summary: Rewrite the README to the ~800-word front-door contract with a regenerable `check` diagnostic as the one hero visual

per.section: The front door — the README is the landing page

new.path: scripts/gen-demo (capture pipeline) + the committed demo asset
new.description: A scripted terminal-capture pipeline that renders a real `miette` `check` diagnostic and its committed output asset, regenerable from the shipped binary — never a hand-curated screenshot that drifts, per the hero-visual Decision (the demo is a projection of real output).

edit.description: Rewrite to the genre-standard short landing page: tagline (category + differentiator — the type checker for the harness, 'makes it good' vs rulesync's 'makes it portable'), badge row, one hero visual (the diagnostic), a highlights list, and a run-before-install `check --harness` quickstart that produces real findings on the reader's own `.claude/`, plus an honest pre-1.0 status line. Everything else links out.

acceptance: README.md is the ~800-word front door with the diagnostic hero visual produced by the captioned command; the demo asset is committed and regenerable; cargo green.
notes: From the offering tranche (specs/55-offering.md). Two Decisions govern: the front-door shape and 'the hero visual is the diagnostic, not a benchmark' — the demo asset must be a committed, regenerable projection of real `check` output (scripted capture), not a drift-prone screenshot. Heavier than the other doc entries because of the capture pipeline. README.md + scripts/ only; disjoint from the rest.

summary: Embed the built-in std-lib from temper's own `packages/` PACKAGE.md sources and retire `contracts/*.toml` — the dogfood un-park that validates the reconciled machinery

per.section: Decision: a package is project-authorable, not vendor-privileged — and is itself a kind

gate.reason: the authored sources must exist first: a human authors the built-in `packages/{skill.anthropic,rule.anthropic}/PACKAGE.md` in temper's product tree (product source, not the dogfood workspace — b0cf244) — the resolved (package-surface-sequencing) order, machinery first / dogfood as validation; last in the chain, needs the surface-language chain (MEMBER-DOCUMENT-IMPORT…KIND-AUTHORED-ARTIFACT + GRAPH-CUSTOM-KIND) shipped, which it now is

new.description: The authored built-in package sources in temper's product tree (`10-contracts.md` names the home as product source, not `.temper/`); the embed target replacing `contracts/*.toml`. The `rule` → `rule.anthropic` rename lands here (`specs/10-contracts.md`, built-ins named for their source).
Embed the authored package directories into the binary — a sanctioned-crate addition (include_dir / build.rs codegen), folded in per the resolved fork.

edit.description: Built-in names resolve from the embedded PACKAGE.md sources instead of `include_str!("../contracts/*.toml")` (main.rs:59/65).
The plugin ships the embedded PACKAGE.md std-lib verbatim instead of the TOML files (bundle.rs:122/126, 210/216).
Retire the TOML-file contract loader once nothing resolves through it.

tests.asserts: built-ins resolve from the embedded authored sources; the bundled plugin carries them; nothing references contracts/*.toml.

acceptance: One artifact, two provenance roles: the same PACKAGE.md is authored as product source and shipped embedded; `contracts/` is gone; the std-lib resolves; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: The dogfood finish of the chain. On disk: contracts/skill.anthropic.toml + contracts/rule.toml still exist, main.rs:59/65 + bundle.rs still include_str! them, only packages/DOSSIER.md authored (no PACKAGE.md product tree). Blocked on human authoring; the embedded contracts floor persists until then by design. PACKAGE-CLAUSE-SOURCE (source-key parse) is now filed as a loader prereq for that authoring session.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: the requirements/satisfies reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; the surface-language chain it waited on (through KIND-AUTHORED-ARTIFACT + GRAPH-CUSTOM-KIND) has now shipped, so the blocker is priority alone — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib (the EMBED-BUILTIN-PACKAGES model — NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction. Shaped to the surface-language forms — one AGENT.md member document, an authored PACKAGE.md, no meta.toml, no contracts/agent.toml. On revival its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry — notably COVERAGE-CUSTOM-KIND, which also edits main.rs.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than blindly clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.
